archetype: Organic & Earthy (Customized based on user explicit preferences)

onboarding_illustration.description: Abstract/minimalist engineering drawing to be used in onboarding as a visual accent (optional).

instructions_to_main_agent.1: Strictly use React Native & Expo. Do not use Next.js, HTML, or Tailwind CSS classes. Use React Native's StyleSheet API.
instructions_to_main_agent.2: Install Expo Google Fonts specifically `@expo-google-fonts/dm-sans` and `@expo-google-fonts/dm-mono` and use `useFonts` hook for loading.
instructions_to_main_agent.3: Implement a solid Theme context (or use React Navigation's DarkTheme/DefaultTheme overrides) to provide automatic switching between light and dark modes based on device system settings. Use `useColorScheme` hook from React Native.
instructions_to_main_agent.4: Use `react-native-chart-kit` for all charts. Dashboard requires: bar chart for budget vs actual, donut charts for expenses and bills.
instructions_to_main_agent.5: UI MUST be completely flat (no shadows/elevation), relying on the 0.5px border and surface/background color contrast to define hierarchy. Use 12px border radius for all cards.
instructions_to_main_agent.6: Typography: Enforce DM Mono exclusively for any textual element representing numbers, currency, or numerical stats. DM Sans for all standard text (labels, headers, descriptions).
instructions_to_main_agent.7: Use MongoDB (Mongoose) for the backend API, and expose standard REST routes using Express (if building a custom backend) or FastAPI if strictly adhering to the tech stack (Wait: user stated MongoDB backend, and system says FastAPI. Implement FastAPI backend with Motor/PyMongo). Provide simple schemas for Bills, Expenses, and User Configuration.
instructions_to_main_agent.8: Include explicit test IDs via `testID` (not `data-testid` in React Native) property on interactive elements for automated testing (e.g., `testID="add-expense-button"`).
instructions_to_main_agent.9: Screens structure: Ensure Setup, Bills, Expenses, Dashboard are configured in the Bottom Tab Navigator. Onboarding, App Settings, and Monthly History should be Stack screens that overlay or navigate from tabs.
